observations.recommendation: We recommended that the non-existing or missing PPE items without available record of accountability be identified and be derecognized from the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the above personnel:
For the Supply Officer:
-Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Water Vehicle.
-Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption.
-Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets and Monthly Fuel Consumption.
For the Drivers
-To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
-To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels where he summarizes in chronological order his trip for the month.
-To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.
For the Chief Accountant
-To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the Drivers to the Auditor.

Therefore, we recommended that Management identify and derecognize the
non-existing or missing PPE items without available record of accountability
from the books of accounts, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of
government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the LCE faithfully:
a) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR, PAR, and ARE
as prescribed;
b) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed
physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE
to the audit team not later than January 31 of succeeding year; and
c) Mandate the Municipal Accountant to recognize the appropriate
depreciation expenses of the PPE accounts.

In adherence to the provisions of Section 20 of MNGAS Volume I, we
recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish a duly certified list showing
the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to
the municipal accountant. Based on the list, the municipal accountant shall
prepare a JEV for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special
education tax receivable at the beginning of the year; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjustment
on the classification of the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special
Education Tax Receivable for the appropriate presentation on the face of
the Statement of Financial Position in accordance with the thresholds
provided by IPSAS 1. Provided further that such adjustments shall be
supported with the reconciling documents from the municipal treasurer.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the following personnel:
For the Supply Officer:
a. Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of
Government Water Vehicle.
b. Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption.
c. Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets and Monthly Fuel
Consumption.
For the Drivers
a. To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
b. To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels where he summarizes in
chronological order his trip for the month.
c. To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.
For the Chief Accountant
a. To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the Drivers to the
Auditor.

Therefore, we recommended that Management cease the use of the old canvass
form and update its documents by using the prescribed RFQ form.

We recommended that the LCE :
a. abide with section 56 of PD 464;
b. intensify the tax campaign for bigger collection; and
c. provide tax incentives through Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the LCE direct the personnel concerned to settle their
respective Notice of Suspension in accordance with Section 9.4 of the COA)
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and
post repair inspection to LGU properties that are subjected to repair and
furnish the reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the date of each
of the inspection activity;
b. Direct the General Service Officer to maintain the records regarding the
history of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims pertaining to repairs
and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment with lacking
supporting documents.

We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to immediately
furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of Acceptance & Inspection Report
within 24 hours after acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after
the inspection and acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items
as required by Section 114 (d) & 116 of COA No.92-386 date October 12, 1992
and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 so that timely
inspection of deliveries can be made by the audit team.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare, update and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting of the account Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account-111. The Municipal Accountant should make a representation, with the approval of the Municipal Mayor, to the agency depository bank for the timely release of the bank statements in order so that BRS can be prepared, updated and submitted on time. The Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency, should see to it that this requirement should be complied by the Municipal Accountant being the responsible officer to prepare the BRS. Moreover, she should initiate efforts to address the problem of the latter by reducing her workloads and delegate them to other personnel capable of doing the job in the finance department. In accordance with Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the Manual;
b) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR, PAR, and ARE as prescribed; and
c) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post repair inspection to LGU properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the date of each of the inspection activity;
b. Direct the General Service Officer to maintain the records regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims pertaining to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment with lacking supporting documents.
We recommend the management to cease the use of the old canvass format and update it documents by using the prescribed RFQ form.
We recommend that the management to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the disbursement vouchers that has not yet submitted.
We recommend that the LCE to instruct the Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer to strictly adhere with the requirements of RA 9994 during planning and implementing phase.
We required the accountable officer to deposit his collections intact and regularly to avoid misuse of funds pursuant to the above-cited regulations.

The LGU- Mamasapano is a consistent calamity inflicted area, be it
man-made and natural, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive
to continue the strengthening of its mitigation or preventive activities
for DRRM especially during the early stage of the pandemic.

Therefore, we recommended that the non-existing or missing PPE
items without available record of accountability be identified and
derecognized from the books of accounts, in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts of government agencies to establish PPE
balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and
accountability.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and
meticulously post the acquisition of PPE to the respective
ledger cards and property cards upon receipt of the source
documents and keep and maintain the same in accordance
with the provisions of the Manual;
b) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR,
PAR, and ARE as prescribed; and
c) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual
detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and
submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than
January 31 of succeeding year.

Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly
direct the concerned personnel to:
a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-
repair inspection and post repair inspection to LGU
properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the
reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the
date of each of the inspection activity;
b. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the records
regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of
the LGU; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims
pertaining to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and
heavy equipment with lacking supporting documents.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the
following personnel:

For the Supply Officer:
a. Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel
Consumption of Government Water Vehicle.
b. Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption.
c. Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets and
Monthly Fuel Consumption.

For the Drivers
a. To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
b. To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels where
he summarizes in chronological order his trip for the
month.
c. To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.

For the Chief Accountant
a. To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the
Drivers to the Auditor.

Therefore, we recommended to the management to cease the use of
the old canvass form and update its documents by using the prescribed RFQ form.

We recommended that the LCE to instruct the Municipal
Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer to strictly adhere
with the requirements of RA 9994 during planning and
implementing phase.

We required the accountable officer to deposit his collections
intact and regularly to avoid misuse of funds pursuant to the above-
cited regulations.

As required by COA Circular 2015-008, we recommended the
Honorable Mayor to:
a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to
conduct the physical count of its Road Network and
prepare a report thereon;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the Local
Road Network account based on the result of the physical
count and disclose the said account in the Notes to
Financial Statements in compliance with Item VI.2 of
COA 2015-008;
c. Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain a
Local Road Inventory and Road Map and prepare a
report thereon at the end of every accounting period as
well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property
Card for all roads and its components; and
d. Require Municipal Engineer to provide the municipal
accountant and the GSO with complete description and
cost of segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to
instruct the Municipal Accountant to depreciate the Property,
Plant and Equipment Account (PPE) which were not depreciated
as presented on Table IV and to instruct the General Services
Officer to submit to the audit team the Report of the Physical
Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE),
pursuant to Section 262 of the Government Accounting Manual
(GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I, Section 156 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October
20, 1992 and Section 124 (2) of the Philippine Public Sector
Accounting System (PPSAS) 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment
(PPE).

For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit
recommendations that the management shall:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to
conduct physical count of PPE and submit the required
inventory report on time for faithful compliance with Sec.
266 of GAM for LGUs Volume 1; and
b. Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the
Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property
ledger cards for reconciliation and control purposes, and
submit the RCPPE per requirements of Sec 269 of GAM for
LGUs Volume I for the Audit Team’s review and
verification.

We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO)
to immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of
Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24 hours after acceptance of all
consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the
LGU personnel concerned on other items as required by Section 114 (d) & 116 of COA No.92-386 date October 12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made by the audit team.

In compliance with the GAM for LGUs, we recommended to the
Honorable Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and maintain the requirements of Chapter 5, Sections
143 to 144, 159 and 161 to 163 of GAM for LGUs.

As required by COA Circular 2015-008, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor shall: a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon; b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the Local Road Network account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Item VI.2 of COA 2015-008; c. Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and d. Require Municipal Engineer to provide the municipal accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost of segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to depreciate the Property, Plant and Equipment Account (PPE) which were not depreciated as presented on Table IV and to instruct the General Services Officer to submit to the audit team the Report of the Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), pursuant to Section 262 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I, Section 156 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 (2) of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting System (PPSAS) 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the management shall: a. Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct physical count of PPE and submit the required inventory report on time for faithful compliance with Sec. 266 of GAM for LGUs Volume 1; and b. Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property ledger cards for reconciliation and control purposes, and submit the RCPPE per requirements of Sec 269 of GAM for LGUs Volume I for the Audit Team’s review and verification.

We recommended to the Honorable Mayor to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant that the municipality should observe the limitations for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous expenses prescribed under Section 325(h) of R.A. 7160. Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant to take necessary adjusting entries to correct the line items on the face of the Statement of Financial Performance in accordance with the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommended that the management shall submit the 20% Development Fund submissions on the structural projects for technical review and inspection by the audit team as well as the Regional Technical Services, Commission on Audit-BARMM, in violation to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known is the Government Auditing Code of Philippines and Section 3.1.4 to 3.1.7 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24 hours after acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items as required by Section 114 (d) & 116 of COA No.92-386 date October 12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made by the audit team.

In compliance with the GAM for LGUs, we recommended to the Honorable Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the requirements of Chapter 5, Sections 143 to 144, 159 and 161 to 163 of GAM for LGUs.

In order to ensure the timely submission of reports and disbursement vouchers, we recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time given by the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension in accordance with Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the management shall:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct physical count of PPE and submit the required inventory report on time for faithful compliance with Sec. 266 of GAM for LGUs Volume 1; and
b. Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property ledger cards for reconciliation and control purposes, and submit the Inventory Report per requirements of Sec 269 of GAM for LGUs. As reconciled, for the Audit Team’s review and verification.
observations.reason: Due to the pandemic, the management has not focused on the said findings as they are busy with their extension of public service.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to:
a. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and movements of the supplies in accordance with Sec. 114, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS).; respectively; and
b. The management shall recognize Office Supplies Inventory at its book first and follow the moving average method for costing in accordance with IPSAS 12, par 44 and sec 115, respectively; and
c. Conduct first physical count at the year-end for faithful compliance with Sec. 124 of NGAS Volume 1.
observations.reason: Due to the pandemic, the management has not focused on the said findings as they are busy with their extension of public service.

observations.recommendation:
In adherence to the provisions of Section 20 of NGAS Volume I, we recommended that the management shall:
a. Require the municipal treasurer to furnish a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the municipal accountant. Based on the list, the municipal accountant shall prepare a JEV for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special education tax receivable at the beginning of the year; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjustment on the classification of the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivable for the appropriate presentation on the face of the Statement of Financial Position in accordance with the thresholds provided by IPSAS 1. Provided further that such adjustments shall be supported with the reconciling documents from the municipal treasurer.

In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and DVs, we recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time given by the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

Recommendation
As required by COA Circular 2015-008, we recommended the Honorable Mayor to:
a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count
of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the Local Road Network account
based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account in the
Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Item VI.2 of COA 2015-008;
c. Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain a Local Road Inventory
and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting
period as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all
roads and its components; and
d. Require Municipal Engineer to provide the municipal accountant and the GSO
with complete description and cost of segregation of road components for road
projects.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to depreciate the Property, Plant and Equipment Account (PPE) which were
not depreciated as presented on Table IV and to instruct the General Services Officer to
submit to the audit team the Report of the Physical Count on the Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE), pursuant to Section 262 of the Government Accounting Manual
(GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I, Section 156 of Commission on Audit (COA)
Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 (2) of the Philippine Public
Sector Accounting System (PPSAS) 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

Recommendation:
For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit recommendations that
the management shall:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct physical count of
PPE and submit the required inventory report on time for faithful compliance
with Sec. 266 of GAM for LGUs Volume 1; and
b. Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the Municipal Accountant
to maintain their respective property ledger cards for reconciliation and control
purposes, and submit the RCPPE per requirements of Sec 269 of GAM for
LGUs Volume I for the Audit Team’s review and verification.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Honorable Mayor to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer
and the Municipal Accountant that the municipality should observe the limitations for
extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses prescribed under Section 325(h) of R.A. 7160.
The team further recommend that the practice of charging miscellaneous expenses be
stopped and must be charged to appropriate accounts, and that expenditures be duly
documented. Furthermore, direct the municipal Accountant to take necessary adjusting
entries to its inappropriately charged accounts to correct the line accounts on the face of the
Statement of Financial Performance in accordance with the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System for LGUs.

Recommendation
We recommended the management to submit the 20% Development Fund on the
structural projects for contract review and inspection, in accordance with Section 122 of the
Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of
the Philippines and Sections 3.1.4 to 3.1.7 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No.
2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Recommendation:
We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to immediately
furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24 hours
after acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and
acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items as required by Section 114 (d)
& 116 of COA No.92-386 date October 12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No. 95-
006 dated May 18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made by the audit
team.

Recommendation
In compliance with the GAM for LGUs, we recommended to the Honorable
Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the
requirements of Chapter 5, Sections 143 to 144, 159 and 161 to 163 of GAM for LGUs.

Recommendation:
In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and DVs, we
recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time given by the
Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules
and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel
concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension in accordance with Section 9.4 of
the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

In order to ensure adherence to the provisions in Section 287 of RA 7160 on the required 20% Development Fund on the purposes for which the fund is intended, it is recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and all officials responsible in the preparation of the budget and utilization of funds shall observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated September 20, 2005 and we are requiring the Municipal Accountant to submit DVs of such allocation.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the DVs

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to the management to submit to the Office of the ATL its Solid Waste Management Plan for faithful adherence to the provisions in Section 16 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the said report.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit
recommendations that the management shall:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct physical
count of PPE and submit the required inventory report on time for
faithful compliance with Sec. 124 of NGAS Volume 1; and
b. Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the Municipal
Accountant to maintain their respective property ledger cards for
reconciliation and control purposes, and submit the Inventory Report
per requirements of Sec 114 of NGAS Volume I, Sec. 14 & 45 of NGAS
Volume II and Sec 490 of GAAM. As reconciled, for the Audit Team’s
review and verification.

Recommendation:
We recommended the management to:
a. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and
movements of the supplies in accordance with Sec. 114, Volume I of the
Manual on the NGAS;
b. The management shall recognize Office Supplies Inventory at its book
first and follow the moving average method for costing in accordance
with IPSAS 12, par 44 and sec 115, respectively; and
c. Conduct physical count at the year-end for faithful compliance with Sec.
124 of NGAS Volume 1.

Recommendation:
In adherence to the provisions of Section 20 of NGAS Volume I, we
recommended that the management shall:
a. Require the municipal treasurer to furnish a duly certified list showing the
names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the
municipal accountant. Based on the list, the municipal accountant shall
prepare a JEV for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special
education tax receivable at the beginning of the year; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjustment on
the classification of the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special
Education Tax Receivable for the appropriate presentation on the face of
the Statement of Financial Position in accordance with the thresholds
provided by IPSAS 1. Provided further that such adjustments shall be
supported with the reconciling documents from the municipal treasurer.

Recommendation:
In order to ensure adherence to the provisions in Section 287 of RA 7160
on the utilization of the required 20% Development Fund on purposes for
which the fund is intended, it is recommended that the Municipal Budget
Officer and all officials responsible in the preparation of the budget and
utilization of funds shall observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated September 20, 2005 and we are
requiring the Municipal Accountant to submit DVs of such allocation.

Recommendation:
It is recommended to the management to submit to the Office of the ATL its
Solid Waste Management Plan for faithful adherence to the provisions in Section
16 of RA 7160.

Recommendation:
In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and DVs, we
recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time given
by the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and other
pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

observations.managementAction: The Management has covered or spent P 14,748,733.64 or 20.02% of the IRA of the Municipality for different Development Projects in line with DILG-DBM joint memorandum circular no. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 dated December 2, 2010.

observations.recommendation: Since LGU-Mamasapano is a consistent calamity inflicted area, be it man-made and natural, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to continue the strengthening of its mitigation or preventive activities for DRRM.

We recognize the efforts of the municipality on the implementation of GAD programs. We recommend that the Management should also implement some programs/activities for women during disaster, calamities and other crisis situations for their GAD programs.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should submit bank monthly and its corresponding bank statements.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the Section 57 of the PD 464. We also advise the LCE to conduct massive tax campaigns and exert extra effort on the collection of RPT. The LCE and SB may initiate to give tax incentives for those who will pay their respective taxes early or even for those who are subject for delinquency.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit JEV to the Provincial Auditor for the purpose of establishment of accounts in their books. Such JEV should be copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Management has strengthened its tax campaign.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of accounts is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: The Management should instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to finish the physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting office for reconciliation of the records.
observations.managementAction: The Management should instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to finish the physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting office for reconciliation of the records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC will prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring report that would represent all procurement activities for each semester and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The LCE/HOPE instructed the BAC to comply the PMR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Disbursing Officer to observe that prompt and timely payment of the mandatory deductions of their organic employees.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should submit pictures of    various projects as supporting documents.
The Management should utilize their 20% Development Fund in accordance    with the above mentioned Memorandum Circular and other pertinent laws.
The Management should also venture on other projects covered or Social Development.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
Since LGU- Mamasapano is a consistent calamity inflicted area, be it man-made and natural, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to continue the strengthening of its mitigation or preventive activities for DRRM. We commend them for their accomplishments in their municipality.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
Management should also implement some of the programs/activities for women during disaster, calamities and other crisis situations for their GAD programs.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
a. Request the Treasurer to retrieve all bank statements for the year 2017 and furnish the same to the Accountant for the preparation of the BRS.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit JEV to the Provincial Auditor for the purpose of establishment of accounts in their books. Such JEV should be copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
The Management should instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to finish the physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting office for reconciliation of the records.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend that the BAC will prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring report that would represent all procurement activities for each semester and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

The Management should instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to finish the physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting office for reconciliation of the records.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted their physical inventory; however it’s not yet finished.
observations.reason: They have not prioritized the physical count of the PPE and poor organization of property records like ledgers and cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management purchase their commonly-used supplies from the Procurement Service of the DBM or through public bidding in compliance with abovementioned law. Emergency items that are not available from the Procurement Service may be purchased thru shopping provided that procurement is supported with BAC Resolution recommending to the Head of Procuring Entity (HOPE) that the mode of procurement for those items shall be thru shopping and a certification from the Procurement Service that the item requested are not available.

We recommend that management should reconstitute a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in conformity to the membership requirement under RA 9003 and it’s IRR in order to optimize the involvement of civil society as envisioned by law
observations.managementAction: Manage

observations.recommendation: a. We recommend that the municipal accountant should submit monthly or at least quarterly monitoring reports on the status of implementation of the fund.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
Management should also implement some of the programs/activities for women during disaster, calamities and other crisis situations for their GAD programs.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should submit pictures of    various projects as supporting documents.
The Management should utilize their 20% Development Fund in accordance    with the above mentioned Memorandum Circular and other pertinent laws.
The Management should also venture on other projects covered or Social Development.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should submit pictures of various projects as supporting documents.
We recommend that the Management should instruct its Local School Board,                         that during the planning and budget preparation for the ensuing year, consider and include also expenditures allocated for public school facilities and equipment; educational research; purchase of books and periodicals; and sports development;
Management should ensure that SEF be expended exclusively for expenditures provided for under joint circular no. 01 series of 1998 issued by DECS, DBM and DILG.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
Management should require the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommend that the Management purchase their commonly-used supplies from the Procurement Service of the DBM or through public bidding in compliance with abovementioned law. Emergency items that are not available from the Procurement Service may be purchased thru shopping provided that procurement is supported with BAC Resolution recommending to the Head of Procuring Entity (HOPE) that the mode of procurement for those items shall be thru shopping and a certification from the Procurement Service that the item requested are not available.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
The management should integrate in their regular activities, programs and projects that enhance the mobility and safety of differently-abled persons and senior citizens in accordance with Section 32 of GAA.
Management should instruct the municipal accountant to submit monthly or at least quarterly, monitoring reports on the status of implementation of the fund.
Management should instruct the general services officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the report on the physical count of PPE.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted their physical inventory; however it’s not yet finished.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and poor organization of property records like ledgers and cards.

observations.recommendation:
Management should optimally utilize its EDF or at least 20% of its IRA.
Management should also venture on the other projects covered or Social Development and Environmental Management.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted report and has incurred disbursement above the threshold 20% of the IRA.

observations.recommendation: a.) Management should strictly observe the due dates for the remittance to GSIS, HDMF and Phil Health in order to update the records of the employees and to avoid any surcharges and penalties that might be imposed due to delay.

observations.recommendation: a.) Management should instruct the personnel concerned to submit the listing of real property tax declarants, amount and due date, also the subsidiary ledger on the Real Property Tax Receivable on General Fund and Special Educational Fund, pursuant to the Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 otherwise known as the State Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the copy of the real property tax declarants list from the office of the assessors.

observations.recommendation:
a.) Management should instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to verify the status of these checks. If these are cancelled checks, submit the original copies of these checks, marked as cancelled to the Audit Team.

b.) Management should instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the Disbursement Vouchers together with the checks.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the following required documents to the Audit Team.

Management should exert efforts in submitting reports within the prescribe periods.

observations.recommendation:
Management should exert efforts in submitting reports within prescribe periods.
